name: bandersnatch
perf_notes: Async concurrent downloader (aiohttp). Concurrency is intentionally capped low (workers default 3; docs warn against exceeding ~10) to stay friendly to PyPI's Fastly CDN. No published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks. Initial full sync transfers the entire PyPI file set; incremental syncs read the changelog/serial and only fetch packages changed since the last stored serial, so steady-state cost tracks PyPI churn rather than total mirror size. Uses the PEP 691 JSON Simple API for package metadata by default since v7.0 (legacy XML-RPC metadata-sync paths removed; XML-RPC is now best-effort only).
scale_notes: Single-process batch job; no HA, clustering, or horizontal scaling. State is a single status/serial file, no database. Routinely mirrors the complete PyPI (millions of files, ~20+ TB of distributions as of the mid-2020s). Filtering plugins (allowlist/blocklist, latest-N releases, size, version-count, prerelease) shrink a partial mirror to a small fraction. bandersnatch only writes a static tree; HTTP serving (and any TLS/auth) is delegated to a separate web server (nginx/Apache) or S3 static hosting.
summary: Open-source PyPA command-line tool that builds and incrementally maintains a complete or filtered static mirror of PyPI (PEP 503 HTML + PEP 691/700 JSON) on local disk or S3-compatible object storage for any web server to serve.
extras: Read-only mirror generator: no upload/publish API, no authentication, no TLS, and no HTTP serving of its own (it only writes a static tree). twine and flit cannot publish to it; a separate web server provides serving/TLS/auth.

Rich filtering plugin system: allowlist/blocklist by project, release, and PEP 440 version specifier; prerelease filter; keep latest-N releases; version-count filter; wheel platform/ABI filter; max-package-size filter; project- and release-file metadata regex filters (a regex metadata filter can match the license classifier, giving a coarse license policy)
delete subcommand removes packages; verify subcommand repairs missing files and garbage-collects unowned files
diff-file generation lists new/changed files for air-gapped, two-stage offline syncs
keep_index_versions retains timestamped prior index files as a rollback safeguard
Mirrors legacy /pypi/<pkg>/json metadata (json=true) alongside the simple index, enabling a statically-served legacy JSON API

Generates PEP 503 HTML and PEP 691/700 JSON simple indexes simultaneously (simple-format = ALL); JSON includes api-version 1.1 with versions, size, and upload-time fields, and data-yanked (PEP 592) is emitted in both formats
Does NOT mirror PEP 658/714 wheel METADATA (no data-core-metadata) or PEP 740 attestations/provenance; core-metadata mirroring remains an open discussion (issue #1124)
Two storage backends in v7.x: local filesystem (default) and S3-compatible object storage via boto3 (bandersnatch[s3]); the legacy OpenStack Swift backend was removed in v7.0
Official Docker image (pypa/bandersnatch) bundling runner.py for scheduled periodic syncs
Cross-platform: Linux, macOS, Windows (Windows requires symlink/elevation privileges)
Per-file SHA256 verification with selectable compare-method (hash vs stat) and digest_name; can consume any PEP 691-advertised digest

v7.0 made the PEP 691 JSON Simple API the default sync method, removed legacy XML-RPC metadata-sync paths and the Swift backend, and requires Python >=3.12; v7.1 added configurable filesystem permission settings
